children.heading: Agency
children.label: 1 Agency
children.text: The term “agency” has the meaning given that term in .section 551(1) of title 5

children.heading: Client
children.label: 2 Client
children.text: The term “client” means any person or entity that employs or retains another person for financial or other compensation to conduct lobbying activities on behalf of that person or entity. A person or entity whose employees act as lobbyists on its own behalf is both a client and an employer of such employees. In the case of a coalition or association that employs or retains other persons to conduct lobbying activities, the client is the coalition or association and not its individual members.

children.label: A
children.text: the President;

children.label: B
children.text: the Vice President;

children.label: C
children.text: any officer or employee, or any other individual functioning in the capacity of such an officer or employee, in the Executive Office of the President;

children.label: D
children.text: any officer or employee serving in a position in level I, II, III, IV, or V of the Executive Schedule, as designated by statute or Executive order;

children.label: E
children.text: any member of the uniformed services whose pay grade is at or above O–7 under ; andsection 201 of title 37

children.label: F
children.text: any officer or employee serving in a position of a confidential, policy-determining, policy-making, or policy-advocating character described in .section 7511(b)(2)(B) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: a Member of Congress;

children.label: B
children.text: an elected officer of either House of Congress;

children.label: i
children.text: a Member of Congress;

children.label: ii
children.text: a committee of either House of Congress;

children.label: iii
children.text: the leadership staff of the House of Representatives or the leadership staff of the Senate;

children.label: iv
children.text: a joint committee of Congress; and

children.label: v
children.text: a working group or caucus organized to provide legislative services or other assistance to Members of Congress; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other legislative branch employee serving in a position described under .section 13101(13) of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: independent contractors; or

children.label: B
children.text: volunteers who receive no financial or other compensation from the person or entity for their services.

children.heading: Foreign entity
children.label: 6 Foreign entity
children.text: The term “foreign entity” means a foreign principal (as defined in section 1(b) of the Foreign Agents Registration Act of 1938 ().22 U.S.C. 611(b)

children.heading: Lobbying activities
children.label: 7 Lobbying activities
children.text: The term “lobbying activities” means lobbying contacts and efforts in support of such contacts, including preparation and planning activities, research and other background work that is intended, at the time it is performed, for use in contacts, and coordination with the lobbying activities of others.

children.label: i
children.text: the formulation, modification, or adoption of Federal legislation (including legislative proposals);

children.label: ii
children.text: the formulation, modification, or adoption of a Federal rule, regulation, Executive order, or any other program, policy, or position of the United States Government;

children.label: iii
children.text: the administration or execution of a Federal program or policy (including the negotiation, award, or administration of a Federal contract, grant, loan, permit, or license); or

children.label: iv
children.text: the nomination or confirmation of a person for a position subject to confirmation by the Senate.

children.label: i
children.text: made by a public official acting in the public official’s official capacity;

children.label: ii
children.text: made by a representative of a media organization if the purpose of the communication is gathering and disseminating news and information to the public;

children.label: iii
children.text: made in a speech, article, publication or other material that is distributed and made available to the public, or through radio, television, cable television, or other medium of mass communication;

children.label: iv
children.text: made on behalf of a government of a foreign country or a foreign political party and disclosed under the Foreign Agents Registration Act of 1938 ( et seq.);22 U.S.C. 611

children.label: v
children.text: a request for a meeting, a request for the status of an action, or any other similar administrative request, if the request does not include an attempt to influence a covered executive branch official or a covered legislative branch official;

children.label: vi
children.text: made in the course of participation in an advisory committee subject to chapter 10 of title 5;

children.label: vii
children.text: testimony given before a committee, subcommittee, or task force of the Congress, or submitted for inclusion in the public record of a hearing conducted by such committee, subcommittee, or task force;

children.label: viii
children.text: information provided in writing in response to an oral or written request by a covered executive branch official or a covered legislative branch official for specific information;

children.label: ix
children.text: required by subpoena, civil investigative demand, or otherwise compelled by statute, regulation, or other action of the Congress or an agency, including any communication compelled by a Federal contract, grant, loan, permit, or license;

children.label: x
children.text: made in response to a notice in the Federal Register, Commerce Business Daily, or other similar publication soliciting communications from the public and directed to the agency official specifically designated in the notice to receive such communications;

children.label: xi
children.text: not possible to report without disclosing information, the unauthorized disclosure of which is prohibited by law;

children.label: I
children.text: a judicial proceeding or a criminal or civil law enforcement inquiry, investigation, or proceeding; or

children.label: II
children.text: a filing or proceeding that the Government is specifically required by statute or regulation to maintain or conduct on a confidential basis,

children.label: xiii
children.text: made in compliance with written agency procedures regarding an adjudication conducted by the agency under or substantially similar provisions;section 554 of title 5

children.label: xiv
children.text: a written comment filed in the course of a public proceeding or any other communication that is made on the record in a public proceeding;

children.label: xv
children.text: a petition for agency action made in writing and required to be a matter of public record pursuant to established agency procedures;

children.label: I
children.text: a covered executive branch official, or

children.label: II
children.text: a covered legislative branch official (other than the individual’s elected Members of Congress or employees who work under such Members’ direct supervision),

children.label: xvii
children.text: a disclosure by an individual that is protected under the amendments made by the Whistleblower Protection Act of 1989, under chapter 4 of title 5, or under another provision of law;

children.label: I
children.text: a church, its integrated auxiliary, or a convention or association of churches that is exempt from filing a Federal income tax return under paragraph 2(A)(i) of , orsection 6033(a) of title 26

children.label: II
children.text: a religious order that is exempt from filing a Federal income tax return under paragraph (2)(A)(iii) of such section 6033(a); and

children.label: I
children.text: officials of a self-regulatory organization (as defined in section 3(a)(26) of the Securities Exchange Act []) that is registered with or established by the Securities and Exchange Commission as required by that Act [ et seq.] or a similar organization that is designated by or registered with the Commodities Future Trading Commission as provided under the Commodity Exchange Act [ et seq.]; and15 U.S.C. 78c(a)(26)15 U.S.C. 78a7 U.S.C. 1

children.label: II
children.text: the Securities and Exchange Commission or the Commodities Future Trading Commission, respectively;

children.heading: Lobbying firm
children.label: 9 Lobbying firm
children.text: The term “lobbying firm” means a person or entity that has 1 or more employees who are lobbyists on behalf of a client other than that person or entity. The term also includes a self-employed individual who is a lobbyist.

children.heading: Lobbyist
children.label: 10 Lobbyist
children.text: The term “lobbyist” means any individual who is employed or retained by a client for financial or other compensation for services that include more than one lobbying contact, other than an individual whose lobbying activities constitute less than 20 percent of the time engaged in the services provided by such individual to that client over a 3-month period.

children.heading: Media organization
children.label: 11 Media organization
children.text: The term “media organization” means a person or entity engaged in disseminating information to the general public through a newspaper, magazine, other publication, radio, television, cable television, or other medium of mass communication.

children.heading: Member of Congress
children.label: 12 Member of Congress
children.text: The term “Member of Congress” means a Senator or a Representative in, or Delegate or Resident Commissioner to, the Congress.

children.heading: Organization
children.label: 13 Organization
children.text: The term “organization” means a person or entity other than an individual.

children.heading: Person or entity
children.label: 14 Person or entity
children.text: The term “person or entity” means any individual, corporation, company, foundation, association, labor organization, firm, partnership, society, joint stock company, group of organizations, or State or local government.

children.label: i
children.text: a college or university;

children.label: ii
children.text: a government-sponsored enterprise (as defined in );section 622(8) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a public utility that provides gas, electricity, water, or communications;

children.label: iv
children.text: a guaranty agency (as defined in ), including any affiliate of such an agency; orsection 1085(j) of title 20

children.label: v
children.text: an agency of any State functioning as a student loan secondary market pursuant to ;section 1085(d)(1)(F) of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: a Government corporation (as defined in );section 9101 of title 31

children.label: C
children.text: an organization of State or local elected or appointed officials other than officials of an entity described in clause (i), (ii), (iii), (iv), or (v) of subparagraph (A);

children.label: D
children.text: an Indian tribe (as defined in ;section 5304(e) of title 251So in original. A closing parenthesis probably should precede the semicolon.1

children.label: E
children.text: a national or State political party or any organizational unit thereof; or

children.label: F
children.text: a national, regional, or local unit of any foreign government, or a group of governments acting together as an international organization.

children.heading: State
children.label: 16 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, and any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.
